title: trigonella spruneriana subsp. mesopotamica
intro: Trigonella spruneriana subsp. mesopotamica, also known as Mesopotamian fenugreek, is a subspecies of the fenugreek plant (Trigonella foenum-graecum) native to the Middle East region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic properties. The health benefits of Mesopotamian fenugreek include reducing blood sugar levels, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable herb for managing various health conditions. Mesopotamian fenugreek is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or powder, and is also used in traditional cooking, particularly in Middle Eastern cuisine.
